observe: Not everyone gets the same number of frames up here. Someone decides the split — fixed, or based on need.
fault: One process is holding almost every frame. The rest are trapped thrashing on what's left.
repair: Rebalance it. Watch what a fairer split actually does.
escape: Keep the allocation fair — an imbalance here compounds fast.

observe: Frame allocation can be fixed or variable across processes.
fault: An imbalanced frame allocation is causing excessive paging.
repair: Adjust the frame allocation across processes.
escape: Confirm a balanced allocation before time expires.

hints: Too few frames for a process's working set is what triggers constant paging.
